include_informational.marker: VERIFIED — existing precedent for default-false opt-in flag (closest analog to the smell-suppression flag this milestone adds)

analyze_rust_file_emit_categories.marker: VERIFIED via direct read of L494-L675

triggers_in_emission_order.title: Unsafe Block Without Safety Rationale
triggers_in_emission_order.rationale: Unsafe blocks without SAFETY comments are documented security-relevant findings; ECONOMIC value to project consumers; NOT a smell. Stays unconditionally emitted.

triggers_in_emission_order.title: Risky transmute Usage
triggers_in_emission_order.rationale: transmute is a documented memory-safety hazard. Stays unconditionally emitted.

triggers_in_emission_order.title: Potential Panic From unwrap()
triggers_in_emission_order.default_emit_post_milestone: FALSE — this is the trigger this milestone gates
triggers_in_emission_order.rationale: Per rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 R2 sub-elephant: production Rust codebases flood with per-.unwrap() Panic findings. The is_rust_test_file mask only covers /tests/, _test.rs, tests.rs paths — does NOT suppress src/lib.rs or src/main.rs unwraps in fixture/example code or in calls that are statically known-safe (.unwrap_or(default), regex literals, lazy_static). Severity Medium plus 0.70 confidence + lack of taint cross-reference means default emission is noise, not signal.

triggers_in_emission_order.title: SQL String Interpolation
triggers_in_emission_order.note: Already overlap-deduped against canonical taint pipeline per dedupe_overlap (vuln.rs:457-464). Out of scope for this milestone.

is_rust_test_file_behavior.marker: VERIFIED via direct read of L724-L730
is_rust_test_file_behavior.current_use: Only consulted by trigger T4 (Panic). T1, T2, T3, T5, T6, T7 emit unconditionally regardless of test-file status.
is_rust_test_file_behavior.documented_limitation: Does NOT suppress in src/lib.rs unwraps, in #[cfg(test)] mod tests blocks within source files, or in build.rs / examples/. R2 sub-elephant flagged this for hardening.

test_inventory.marker: VERIFIED via direct read of L969-L1083

test_inventory_full.name: test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code
test_inventory_full.regression_risk: HIGH — this test asserts findings.iter().any(|f| f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic). If analyze_rust_file's signature is unchanged but the Panic emission is gated at the analyze_file (caller) layer, this test still passes (it calls analyze_rust_file directly). If gating is inside analyze_rust_file via an added parameter, the test must pass include_smells=true to keep asserting.

test_inventory.regression_risk_summary: Of 6 test_analyze_rust_* tests, only 1 (test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code, line 1057) asserts Panic. The other 5 assert UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/SqlInjection/CommandInjection — all unaffected by Panic gating. So the test-update blast radius is exactly 1 of 9 tests.

production.context: analyze_file body inside `if is_rust { ... }` branch — production dispatch

call_sites_of_analyze_rust_file.external_consumers: None — analyze_rust_file is a private (non-pub) fn within commands::remaining::vuln. Its signature can change without breaking the public API.

rationale.A_selected: Add a single bool flag `--include-smells` (default false). When false, suppress only the Panic emission (the singular smell). When true, emit Panic findings as today. UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Sql/Cmd remain ALWAYS emitted. Justification: (1) flag count minimal — VulnArgs already has 7 fields, adding 1 keeps cognitive load low. (2) Panic is the SOLE smell-class trigger per audit above. (3) Closest analog precedent in the same struct: include_informational (line 92) — same pattern (default-false opt-in, semantic gating, applied during filter pipeline at L194-196). (4) The other 6 triggers are documented security findings, not smells; gating them would lose signal. (5) This matches the user's R2 sub-elephant framing — 'production Rust codebases flood with per-.unwrap() Panic findings'. The gripe is specifically about Panic, not UnsafeCode/MemorySafety.
rationale.B_rejected: Tier the smells via --smell-level={none,low,high}. Adds an enum and 3 states for a system with exactly 1 smell trigger. Over-engineered for current scope. If future smell triggers land (e.g., expect-without-context, panic!()), revisit and migrate the bool to a tier enum. Not now.
rationale.C_rejected: Delete Panic emission entirely from analyze_rust_file. Removes a feature; one of the 6 test_analyze_rust_* tests asserts it (test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code at L1057). Per user's no-gaming rule, deleting a real feature to fix a UX issue is too aggressive. Also: the Panic emission has legitimate value when scoped to specific files; total deletion forecloses that.
rationale.D_rejected: Fold Panic emission into a tainted-only trigger via cross-reference with the canonical taint pipeline. This is genuinely the right long-term fix, but scope is huge: requires building a taint-aware unwrap detector (canonical pipeline doesn't currently track unwrap as a panic-sink), threading taint state into analyze_rust_file, and likely adding a new TaintSinkType. Per the task brief: 'Largest scope; closest to a real fix.' Defer to a separate post-publish hardening milestone (could be panic-taint-cross-ref-v1). Not THIS milestone.

candidates.name: --include-smells
candidates.rationale: Mirrors --include-informational precedent (same default-false bool semantic, same struct, same filter-pipeline pattern). 'smells' is industry-standard terminology (cf. tldr smells command at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/*.rs). Singular flag for singular emission category.

candidates.name: --include-panic
candidates.rationale: Too narrow — couples flag name to current single-smell. If future smell triggers land, flag must be deprecated. --include-smells is forward-compatible.

candidates.name: --smell-level
candidates.rationale: Enum overkill for 1 trigger. Reconsider if/when smell trigger count reaches ≥3.

candidates.name: --no-suppress-panic
candidates.rationale: Double-negative confusing. Default-false opt-in is the established pattern.

options_considered.rationale: Mirrors include_informational filter at L194-196. Keeps analyze_rust_file's signature stable — the 6 existing #[test] fns continue passing without modification (they call analyze_rust_file directly, which still emits Panic; the filter only applies in production via VulnArgs::run). ZERO test signature churn for 5 of 6 tests; the 1 test that asserts Panic (test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code) also passes unchanged because it calls analyze_rust_file directly. NEGATIVE side: a pure-unit-test gap — no test exercises the filter at the VulnArgs layer with --include-smells false. Add a NEW test that exercises VulnArgs::run with both flag values to close that gap.

options_considered.approach: fn analyze_rust_file(path, source, include_panic_emit: bool) — caller plumbs it from VulnArgs
options_considered.rationale: Forces all 6 test call sites to update their signature. Higher churn. ALSO: changes private fn signature unnecessarily — the gating is a CLI-layer concern, not an analyzer-layer concern. Wrong abstraction.

smell_classification_helper.rationale: Tight predicate. Currently the ONLY Panic emission from analyze_rust_file uses the title 'Potential Panic From unwrap()' (vuln.rs:580). The vuln_type-only check would over-match if a future canonical taint pipeline ever emits VulnType::Panic — title prefix ensures we only suppress line-scanner Panic. Title-prefix check keeps coupling tight.

json_schema_invariance.what_changes: Per-invocation `findings` array length decreases when --include-smells is omitted (default). Summary counts decrease accordingly (total, severity histogram). Schema is identical; only emission count drops.
json_schema_invariance.downstream_consumer_impact: JSON consumers parsing per-finding shape see no breakage. Consumers comparing finding COUNTS across pre/M2 and post/M2 invocations (e.g., CI baseline trackers, finding-count assertions) WILL see a delta on Rust files with .unwrap() calls. M3 documentation must enumerate the count delta with example before/after on a representative Rust file.
json_schema_invariance.sarif_schema_invariance: SARIF rules array enumerated from VulnFinding records. With Panic suppressed, the 'CWE-703' rule entry will not appear in rules[] when no Panic findings emerge. Schema is unchanged; presence/absence of rule entries reflects findings present. NOT a schema change.

open_uncertainties_marked_INFERRED.claim: M3 binary smoke on a real-world Rust crate (e.g., the tldr-code workspace itself) will show a >50% drop in total finding count when --include-smells is omitted
open_uncertainties_marked_INFERRED.verification_plan: M3 runs `tldr vuln crates/ --lang rust` twice (default + --include-smells) and records counts to M3-binary-smoke.json. The R2 sub-elephant claim of 'flood' will be quantified there.

open_uncertainties_marked_INFERRED.claim: No external CHANGELOG-documented behavior depends on Panic emission count
open_uncertainties_marked_INFERRED.marker: INFERRED — grep over CHANGELOG.md at planning time
open_uncertainties_marked_INFERRED.verification_plan: M2 worker greps CHANGELOG.md for 'Panic' / 'unwrap' / 'CWE-703' references; if found, M2 documents the precedent in M2-report.json and confirms the new flag doesn't violate it.
